verbatim_text: So mu at least has a very strong preference over operations generated by this algorithm. Some highly preferred, some not so much. So this gives us a good learning signal. Yes. When you're doing the variance, are you thinking over the randomness in the LLM or randomness over the states? And if over the states, what's the distribution? This distribution is over the operations generated by this original algorithm. So it's an expectation over this choice of O. O is the random choice here. And given mu and given O, then this variance quantity is fixed. Because mu is fixed, you can just compute. So the two quantities that are random here is mu and O. And so if you're computing the variance, the only thing left random is O. Then that you can compute the expectation over the operation sample by the original algorithm.
